task_completion.description: Image is spatially calibrated to 2 pixels per 1 µm.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Fiji/ImageJ window header showing image dimensions in physical units (e.g., 300x206 µm) instead of pixels, or the 'Set Scale' dialog showing 2.0 pixels/µm (or 0.5 µm/pixel).

task_completion.description: Horizontal intensity line profile across the middle is extracted and saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'horizontal_profile.csv' is visible in the ~/Fiji_Data/results/surface/ directory, or a plot window for the horizontal profile is visible in the Fiji GUI.

task_completion.description: Vertical intensity line profile across the middle is extracted and saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'vertical_profile.csv' is visible in the ~/Fiji_Data/results/surface/ directory, or a plot window for the vertical profile is visible in the Fiji GUI.

task_completion.description: Surface roughness parameters are calculated and saved to a text report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'roughness_report.txt' is visible in the results directory. If open, its contents include the keys Ra, Rq, Rz, Mean_height, and Median_height with values matching or very close to the ground truth.

task_completion.description: A 3D surface plot is generated and saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'surface_plot_3d.png' is visible in the results directory, or an open image viewer/Fiji window shows a 3D topographical representation of the sample.

task_completion.description: An annotated version of the original image with a 50 µm scale bar is saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'annotated_image.png' is visible in the results directory, showing the original grayscale image with a clearly drawn scale bar labeled '50 µm'.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply type or echo the ground truth values into the text file without performing the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for evidence of Fiji/ImageJ usage (e.g., open application, measurement windows, macro execution, or a Python script) rather than a simple terminal 'echo' or text editor typing of the final values.

integrity.description: The 3D surface plot corresponds to the actual image data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D plot should visually match the features of the Au-Pb-Sn solder sample image (peaks and valleys corresponding to the light and dark regions of the original image), not a generic or unrelated 3D plot.

integrity.description: The scale bar was generated using image processing tools, not just arbitrarily drawn.
integrity.visual_evidence: The scale bar should look like a standard Fiji/ImageJ scale bar (usually a solid line with text on top or below) and its length should be proportional to the image (exactly 100 pixels wide for the 600 pixel wide image).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input image is 600x412 pixels. Spatial calibration is 0.5 µm/pixel (2 pixels/µm), making the physical dimensions 300x206 µm. A 50 µm scale bar should be exactly 100 pixels wide (1/6th of the image width). Ground truth roughness parameters (where 1 intensity unit = 1 nm): Ra ≈ 66.93, Rq ≈ 73.25, Rz = 255.0, Mean_height ≈ 125.9, Median_height = 130.0. The 3D surface plot should reflect the topography of the Au-Pb-Sn solder sample.
